title: Shahnameh: The Creation of the World and Gayomart
source: British Library Or. 12688; Florence Magl. III.24
description: The Persian national epic: the creation of the world, the first man Gayomart, and the eternal struggle of good against evil.
introduction: The Shahnameh (Book of Kings) is the longest poem ever written by a single author — 50,000 couplets composed over 30 years. Ferdowsi's masterpiece preserves the mythological and historical memory of the Iranian world from the creation through the Arab conquest. It opens with the creation of the world, the first man Gayomart (cognate with the Avestan Gayomaretan), and establishes the cosmic struggle between the forces of light and darkness that structures all of Iranian civilization.

translation.original_text: سخن هر چه گویم همه گفته‌اند / بر باغ دانش همه رفته‌اند
translation.text: Whatever I say has already been said; all have walked in the garden of knowledge.

translation.original_text: نخست آفرینش خرد را شناس / نگهبان جان است و آن سه پاس
translation.text: Know that the first creation was Wisdom — the guardian of the soul and its threefold watch.
